name: Salesforce
one_liner: Enterprise CRM platform for sales, service, and marketing automation.
self_serve_notes: Free 30-day Developer Edition org gives instant OAuth-based API access via a Connected/External Client App.

api_surface.notes: REST, SOAP, Bulk, and GraphQL APIs cover nearly every CRM object plus SOQL/SOSL queries, Apex, and Flow actions.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio Salesforce toolkit has 179 tools/7 triggers; Salesforce also ships its own hosted Agentforce MCP servers (GA April 2026).

name: HubSpot
one_liner: CRM and marketing/sales/service hub for inbound growth teams.
self_serve_notes: Free HubSpot account plus a free developer test account lets anyone create a private app token or OAuth app instantly.

api_surface.notes: Broad REST API covering CRM objects, marketing, CMS, and workflows; ~244 actions surfaced via Composio.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio HubSpot toolkit (244 tools, 2 triggers); HubSpot also runs its own official remote MCP server (GA April 2026) and a local Developer MCP server.

name: Pipedrive
one_liner: Sales-pipeline-focused CRM for deal tracking and pipeline automation.
self_serve_notes: Free trial account provides an API token instantly from user settings; OAuth requires registering an app in the developer sandbox.

api_surface.notes: Broad REST API (deals, leads, persons, organizations, activities, products); Composio exposes 398 tools.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio Pipedrive toolkit (398 tools/3 triggers); Pipedrive also launched its own native MCP server (June 2026) available on all plans with OAuth setup.

name: Attio
one_liner: Modern, flexible relationship-data CRM built around a customizable data model.
self_serve_notes: API/access-token generation is available on all plans, including free, from Workspace settings by an admin.

api_surface.notes: REST API covers records, lists, attributes, tasks, notes, comments, and webhooks across all objects; Composio exposes 99 tools.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio Attio toolkit (99 tools); Attio's own developer platform explicitly lists an official MCP offering alongside its REST API and SQL interface.

name: Twenty
one_liner: Open-source, self-hostable CRM alternative to Salesforce.
self_serve_notes: Fully open source (self-host free) or managed cloud signup; API keys are created instantly from Settings without any approval.

api_surface.notes: Core API exposes both /rest/ and /graphql/ for CRUD across 30+ standard and custom objects, plus a separate Metadata API for schema.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No official Composio toolkit page found (404); a community-built bash/curl 'agent skill' toolkit for Twenty exists (396 scripts) rather than an official Composio or Twenty MCP server.

name: Podio
one_liner: Customizable workspace/CRM platform for building flexible business apps.
self_serve_notes: Free signup, register an app in account settings to get Client ID/Secret and use OAuth2 (server-side, client-side, password, or app-auth flows).

api_surface.notes: REST API centered on generic 'items' within customizable apps rather than a dedicated CRM object model; Podio itself is in maintenance mode with limited new development.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No Composio Podio toolkit found; only an unrelated third-party 'ProcFu' automation add-on for Podio surfaced, no official MCP server identified.

main_blocker: No composio/official MCP toolkit; must build directly against a dated, generic-object API

name: Zoho CRM
one_liner: Cloud CRM for lead, deal, and contact management within the Zoho suite.
self_serve_notes: Free Zoho account plus Zoho Developer Console 'Self Client' lets a developer generate OAuth credentials immediately, no approval needed.

api_surface.notes: V8 REST API covers Leads, Contacts, Deals, and all CRM modules with metadata, search, and bulk endpoints.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio's 'Zoho' toolkit (12 tools) spans the Zoho suite including CRM actions; Zoho also runs its own official pre-built CRM MCP server.

name: Close
one_liner: Inside-sales CRM with built-in calling, email, and SMS automation.
self_serve_notes: Free trial account, API key generated instantly from account settings with no approval step.

api_surface.notes: REST API covers leads, contacts, opportunities, calls, SMS, tasks; Composio exposes only 6 tools, a narrower slice than Salesforce/HubSpot/Pipedrive.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio Close toolkit (6 tools, API key auth); Close also runs its own official hosted MCP server (mcp.close.com).

name: Copper
one_liner: Google-Workspace-native CRM for relationship and pipeline management.
self_serve_notes: Free trial account; API key generated instantly in System Settings, though OAuth2 is reserved for approved partner integrations.

api_surface.notes: Dev API covers core objects (people, companies, opportunities, activities) with simple token-header auth; rate-limited to 180 req/min.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No Composio Copper toolkit found; no official Copper MCP server found.

main_blocker: No existing Composio/official MCP integration; must build directly against a narrower, older-style API

name: DealCloud
one_liner: Deal/relationship management CRM for private equity, banking, and M&A firms.
self_serve_notes: Only available to existing licensed customers; a site admin must grant a user 'API' capability before that user can enable and copy an API key/secret.

api_surface.notes: Per-site REST API with Swagger UI covering entries, lists, users, and management endpoints, but usable only within an existing customer's provisioned site.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No Composio DealCloud toolkit found and no official MCP server identified; DealCloud is a niche enterprise vertical CRM (Intapp) sold via direct sales, not self-signup.

main_blocker: Requires being an existing paying customer with admin-granted API capability; no free/trial signup

name: Zendesk
one_liner: Cloud-based customer service and support ticketing platform.
self_serve_notes: Developers can sign up for a free 14-day trial account and immediately generate API tokens or OAuth credentials from Admin Center.

api_surface.notes: Extensive REST API covering tickets, users, orgs, macros, automations, custom objects, and search.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a Zendesk toolkit with 452 tools and 2 triggers using OAuth2.

name: Intercom
one_liner: Customer messaging platform combining live chat, help desk, and tickets.
self_serve_notes: Developer Hub issues an Access Token immediately upon creating an app for your own workspace, no approval needed.

api_surface.notes: Broad REST API for contacts, conversations, tickets, articles, data events, and analytics.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists an Intercom toolkit with 133 tools using OAuth2.

name: Freshdesk
one_liner: Cloud help desk software for ticketing, automation, and knowledge base.
self_serve_notes: API key is visible in Profile Settings immediately, including on free trial accounts (with lower rate limits).

api_surface.notes: REST API covers tickets, contacts, companies, solutions/knowledge base, automations, SLAs, and time tracking.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a Freshdesk toolkit with 178 tools using Basic Auth credentials.

name: Front
one_liner: Shared inbox and customer communication hub for support teams.
self_serve_notes: API tokens can be generated freely in account settings for a single-instance integration; OAuth/public app distribution needs Front's app framework and possible exception approval.

api_surface.notes: REST API for conversations, messages, contacts, tags, channels, and rules.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio has a Front toolkit/auth-config but notes actions are 'coming soon', so tool coverage is currently narrow/incomplete.

main_blocker: Composio toolkit tools still limited (coming soon)

name: Pylon
one_liner: Modern B2B customer support and ticketing platform for SaaS companies.
self_serve_notes: CORRECTED after verification: pricing page does reference a free trial, but exact terms/length aren't published and all listed tiers require payment with 3+ seat minimums; an admin can generate API tokens once subscribed.

api_surface.notes: REST API for issues, accounts, and contacts; documented but smaller surface than legacy helpdesks.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Pylon runs its own official remote MCP server (mcp.usepylon.com) with OAuth; no Composio toolkit found (404 on guessed slug).

main_blocker: No free trial; requires paid annual contract

name: LiveAgent
one_liner: Multichannel help desk software with live chat, ticketing, and call center.
self_serve_notes: Admins generate an API key in Configuration > System > API on any account, including trials.

api_surface.notes: REST API v3 documented per-instance covering tickets, contacts, agents, and devices.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists LiveAgent with API_KEY auth but currently zero tools/triggers implemented.

main_blocker: Composio toolkit has no tools yet; would need direct API integration

name: Plain
one_liner: API-first customer support platform built around GraphQL for B2B SaaS.
self_serve_notes: Create a machine user and generate a scoped API key directly from workspace Settings, no approval needed.

api_surface.notes: Full GraphQL API and schema for threads, customers, companies, and tiers, designed API-first.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a Plain toolkit with 22 tools using API key auth, including raw GraphQL query support.

name: Help Scout
one_liner: Email-based shared inbox and help desk for small-to-mid customer support teams.
self_serve_notes: Any user can create a My App from their profile and use the Client Credentials flow to get a bearer token without external approval.

api_surface.notes: Mailbox API covers conversations, customers, mailboxes, tags, and workflows over HTTPS-only OAuth2.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Guessed Composio slugs (help-scout, helpscout) both 404'd; a search snippet claimed a listing but could not be verified on an actual page.

name: Gorgias
one_liner: Helpdesk built for ecommerce brands, integrating deeply with Shopify and social channels.
self_serve_notes: Private-app API key with HTTP Basic Auth is available directly from account settings; public apps distributed to other users require OAuth2 and Gorgias approval.

api_surface.notes: REST API for tickets, customers, teams, tags, and account settings.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a Gorgias toolkit with 32 tools, supporting both API Key and OAuth2.

name: Gladly
one_liner: Enterprise customer-centric contact center platform unifying voice, chat, and email.
self_serve_notes: No public free trial or self-serve signup; access is sales-led with custom annual contracts, though existing customer admins can self-serve API tokens once onboarded.

api_surface.notes: REST API documented for conversations, customers, and webhooks, with a sandbox environment for existing customers.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Guessed Composio slug (gladly) 404'd and no confirmation found elsewhere; no official MCP server found in docs.

main_blocker: Contact-sales onboarding, no public trial access

name: Slack
one_liner: Channel-based team chat and workflow platform with an extensive developer API.
self_serve_notes: Anyone can create a Slack app at api.slack.com/apps and self-serve OAuth2 credentials instantly, free.

api_surface.notes: Web API covers messaging, channels, users, files, reactions, admin, and more with hundreds of methods.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio Slack toolkit has 167 tools/9 triggers; official MCP reference server plus many community MCP servers exist.

name: Twilio
one_liner: Cloud communications platform for programmable SMS, voice, and messaging APIs.
self_serve_notes: Free trial signup on twilio.com immediately gives Account SID/Auth Token and API key creation in the Console.

api_surface.notes: Over 1,800 endpoints across 30+ products (SMS, Voice, WhatsApp, Video, Verify, etc).

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio Twilio toolkit exists. CLARIFIED after verification: Twilio's own mcp.twilio.com server is a read-only docs/spec search tool ("does not execute API calls on your behalf") — the separate twilio-labs/mcp project is the one that actually calls the API; the initial agent pass conflated the two.

name: Zoho Cliq
one_liner: Team chat app from Zoho with bots, webhooks, and REST API for automation.
self_serve_notes: Free Zoho account lets developers generate webhook tokens or OAuth2 client credentials in the Cliq platform console instantly.

api_surface.notes: REST API v2/v3 plus platform features (bots, slash commands, widgets, functions) but narrower/less standard than major chat platforms.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No dedicated Composio Zoho Cliq toolkit found (404); Zoho itself ships an official Zoho MCP server covering Cliq among other Zoho apps.

main_blocker: No off-the-shelf Composio toolkit; must build against Cliq's narrower REST/webhook API

name: Lark (Larksuite)
one_liner: All-in-one enterprise collaboration suite with chat, docs, and open platform APIs.
self_serve_notes: Developers must belong to an enterprise tenant (create or be invited) and self-built apps need tenant admin approval before use, though no Lark platform review is required internally.

api_surface.notes: Open Platform covers Messenger, Docs, Base, Sheets, Calendar, Mail, Tasks, Meetings with granular per-scope permission configuration.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No Composio toolkit found (404); Lark publishes an official CLI/agent-skills tool and an official OpenClaw plugin that function like MCP-style agent integrations.

main_blocker: Requires enterprise tenant + admin approval to install self-built apps; no Composio toolkit

name: Pumble
one_liner: Free team chat app (by CAKE.com) with channels, DMs, and a lightweight REST API.
self_serve_notes: Any user can install the free API-keys addon and generate a key instantly via a slash command, no approval needed.

api_surface.notes: Swagger-documented API covers messaging, reactions, channels, and DMs but is a small action set compared to major platforms.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No Composio toolkit (404); at least one community MCP server for Pumble exists.

main_blocker: Narrow API surface and no Composio toolkit

name: Discord
one_liner: Community/social chat platform with voice, servers, and an extensive bot/OAuth API.
self_serve_notes: Free instant self-serve app/bot creation via the Discord Developer Portal, no approval for basic use.

api_surface.notes: Broad REST API plus Gateway (WebSocket) covering servers, channels, messages, moderation, voice, and more.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio has both a Discord toolkit (28 tools/1 trigger) and a Discord Bot toolkit; many community MCP servers also exist.

name: Telegram
one_liner: Cloud messaging app with a simple, free HTTP Bot API for automation.
self_serve_notes: Free, instant bot token issuance via BotFather chat, no signup form or approval needed.

api_surface.notes: HTTP-based Bot API with a moderate but bounded method set (messages, chats, media, updates); full client API (MTProto) is separate and heavier.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio Telegram toolkit lists 18 tools; several community MCP servers for Telegram also exist.

name: WhatsApp Business
one_liner: Meta's WhatsApp Cloud API for business messaging, templates, and automation.
self_serve_notes: A Meta developer account and app can be created for free with a temporary token for testing, but production use needs Meta Business verification, a permanent system-user token, and message-template approval.

api_surface.notes: Focused messaging API (send/receive messages, templates, media, business profile) rather than a broad platform.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio has a WhatsApp toolkit (Business API only, not personal WhatsApp); numerous community MCP servers exist for the Cloud API.

main_blocker: Meta Business verification and template/phone-number approval needed for production sending

name: Aircall
one_liner: Cloud-based business phone system with call, SMS, and CRM-integration APIs.
self_serve_notes: Existing Aircall customers can self-serve generate Basic Auth API keys in Company Settings, but that requires an active paid Aircall subscription; OAuth2 (for marketplace partner apps) needs Aircall partner approval.

api_surface.notes: Covers calls, contacts, users, numbers, and webhooks — a focused telephony API rather than a broad platform.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Aircall listed in Composio's toolkit catalog but no confirmed dedicated toolkit page found; an unofficial third-party MCP server exists, explicitly not built/endorsed by Aircall.

main_blocker: Basic Auth API keys require an existing paid Aircall account; OAuth2 route requires partner approval

name: Vonage
one_liner: Programmable communications platform for SMS, voice, video, and verification APIs.
self_serve_notes: Free self-serve signup grants an API key/secret plus free trial credit immediately from the developer dashboard.

api_surface.notes: Broad set of communications APIs: SMS, Voice, Video, Verify, Number Insight, Messages, and more.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No Composio Vonage toolkit found (404); Vonage itself ships multiple official MCP servers (documentation MCP server, API bindings/tooling MCP server, telephony MCP server).

main_blocker: No Composio toolkit yet, though Vonage's own official MCP servers cover much of the gap

name: Google Ads
one_liner: Manage and analyze Google Ads campaigns, ad groups, and reporting programmatically.
self_serve_notes: A developer token can be obtained instantly for Test Account access, but production ('Explorer'/Basic) access requires a Google review of the developer token application.

api_surface.notes: Full campaign/ad group/reporting (GAQL) API via google-ads-api with gRPC/REST bindings and extensive resource coverage.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a Google Ads toolkit (OAuth2, ~9 tools); several community MCP servers for Google Ads also exist.

main_blocker: Developer token review needed for production/live account access

name: Meta Ads
one_liner: Create, manage and analyze Facebook/Instagram ad campaigns via the Marketing API.
self_serve_notes: A developer can create a Meta app and get a system-user token to manage their own ad account without App Review, but App Review + Business Verification is required to manage other businesses' ad accounts or exceed default access tiers.

api_surface.notes: Marketing API covers campaigns, ad sets, ads, creatives, custom audiences, and insights across Facebook/Instagram.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio has a 'Metaads' toolkit (OAuth2, requires configuring own OAuth credentials); multiple third-party Meta Ads MCP servers exist.

main_blocker: App Review / Business Verification required for access beyond own ad account

name: LinkedIn Ads
one_liner: Manage LinkedIn advertising campaigns, audiences, and conversions via the Marketing/Advertising API.
self_serve_notes: Developers can create an app and generate test tokens for Development tier, but real usage requires applying per-product and upgrading to Standard tier via a video-demo review process.

api_surface.notes: Advertising API, Community Management API, Lead Sync, Conversions, and Events Management APIs are all versioned REST APIs.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio's LinkedIn toolkit (OAuth2, ~22 tools) includes ad targeting facets/audience count tools alongside organic content tools.

main_blocker: Per-product application and Standard-tier review (video demo) required for real ad account access

name: GoHighLevel
one_liner: All-in-one CRM/marketing automation platform for agencies: funnels, calendars, contacts, campaigns.
self_serve_notes: Developers can sign up for a free Marketplace developer account and create a Private or Public app with OAuth2 (PKCE supported) without needing prior approval to start building/testing.

api_surface.notes: v2 API covers contacts, conversations/messaging, calendars, opportunities/pipelines, products, custom objects, and more (Composio lists 218 tools).

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio's Highlevel toolkit (OAuth2, 218 tools, 0 triggers); community/marketplace MCP servers for GoHighLevel also exist.

name: Mailchimp
one_liner: Email marketing and marketing automation platform with campaigns, audiences, and e-commerce data.
self_serve_notes: Any Mailchimp account holder can instantly generate an API key from account settings; free-tier accounts can access the API (capabilities scale with plan).

api_surface.notes: Marketing API covers campaigns, audiences/lists, automations, e-commerce (stores/orders/products), and reporting (Composio lists 272 tools).

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio's Mailchimp toolkit (OAuth2, 272 tools, 4 triggers); an official/community Mailchimp MCP server also exists.

name: Klaviyo
one_liner: Email/SMS marketing automation and customer data platform for e-commerce brands.
self_serve_notes: Any account can instantly create a Private API Key from Settings; OAuth is reserved for tech partners listed in the App Marketplace.

api_surface.notes: Covers profiles/lists/segments, campaigns, flows, events, and catalogs (Composio lists 225 tools).

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio's Klaviyo toolkit (API Key + OAuth2, 225 tools); Klaviyo also has community MCP servers.

name: systeme.io
one_liner: All-in-one funnel builder with email marketing, courses, and affiliate management.
self_serve_notes: Any user (including free-plan accounts) can instantly generate a public API key from their dashboard profile settings (max 3 keys, X-API-Key header).

api_surface.notes: Public API is limited mainly to contacts, tags, and a few other resources; no ads/campaign send endpoints documented.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a 'Systeme Io' toolkit entry (API_KEY auth) but it shows 0 tools/0 triggers, i.e. not actually implemented yet.

main_blocker: Composio toolkit exists only as an empty placeholder; narrow official API surface

name: Pinterest
one_liner: Publish pins/boards and manage Pinterest ad campaigns via the Pinterest API v5.
self_serve_notes: Developers get free Trial access quickly (reviewed daily) for testing, but write operations (pins, ads) and the Ads API require upgrading to Standard access via a demo-video review.

api_surface.notes: API v5 covers pins, boards, and Ads (campaigns, ad groups, ads, analytics, conversions) once Standard access is granted.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio's Pinterest toolkit entry exists in the catalog (OAuth2) but has 0 tools/0 triggers, i.e. not actually usable; not a managed app.

main_blocker: Standard-access review (demo video) required for write/ads endpoints

name: Threads (Meta)
one_liner: Publish and manage posts/replies/insights on Meta's Threads app via the Threads API.
self_serve_notes: A developer can create a Meta app and test with up to 25 accounts in Development mode immediately, but App Review (screencast demo, ~3-5 business days) is required before production use for other users' accounts.

api_surface.notes: Threads API covers publishing (text/image/video/carousel), replies, insights, and profile info — narrower than the broader Meta Marketing API.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No dedicated Threads toolkit found in Composio's catalog as of research; Threads functionality is separate from the Metaads toolkit.

main_blocker: No Composio toolkit yet; App Review required for production/other-user access

name: SendGrid
one_liner: Transactional and marketing email delivery platform with sending, templates, and analytics APIs.
self_serve_notes: Developers can sign up for a free-tier SendGrid account and instantly create an API key with scoped permissions from the dashboard.

api_surface.notes: Covers mail send, templates, contacts/lists, suppression groups, domain auth, subusers, and stats (Composio lists 359 tools).

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio's SendGrid toolkit (API Key auth, 359 tools); Twilio/SendGrid ecosystem also has community MCP servers.

name: Shopify
one_liner: Hosted ecommerce platform for building and managing online stores.
self_serve_notes: Developers get a free Partner account and unlimited free development stores instantly at partners.shopify.com.

api_surface.notes: GraphQL Admin API (recommended) plus legacy REST Admin API, Storefront API, and many app-extension APIs covering products, orders, inventory, customers, fulfillment.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a large Shopify toolkit (300+ tools); Shopify also ships its own official Storefront MCP server live by default on every eligible store since Q1 2026.

name: WooCommerce
one_liner: Open-source WordPress plugin turning a site into an online store.
self_serve_notes: Any WooCommerce store admin can instantly generate a Consumer Key/Secret under WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API, no approval needed.

api_surface.notes: REST API covers products, orders, customers, coupons, refunds, reports, and settings across the self-hosted store.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio has a WooCommerce tools listing; no official WooCommerce MCP server confirmed in docs.

name: BigCommerce
one_liner: SaaS ecommerce platform for building online stores at scale.
self_serve_notes: Developers can spin up a free BigCommerce trial/sandbox store from the partner portal and immediately create store-level API accounts with X-Auth-Token credentials.

api_surface.notes: REST Management API (products, orders, customers, carts, webhooks) plus a GraphQL Storefront API.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Search evidence confirms Composio includes a BigCommerce toolkit in its catalog.

name: Salesforce Commerce Cloud
one_liner: Enterprise B2C/B2B commerce platform (Demandware) for large-scale online retail.
self_serve_notes: No self-serve signup; a trial sandbox requires contacting your Commerce Cloud Account Executive or Partner Manager, and production access requires being an existing licensed customer/partner.

api_surface.notes: SCAPI (current, OAuth2/SLAS-secured Shopper and Admin APIs) has replaced the deprecated OCAPI; covers catalog, orders, customers, pricing, and shopper flows.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio has generic 'Salesforce' CRM toolkits but no dedicated Commerce Cloud/B2C Commerce toolkit found.

main_blocker: no self-serve sandbox; requires account exec/partner manager approval

name: Magento (Adobe Commerce)
one_liner: Ecommerce platform (open-source Magento or paid Adobe Commerce/SaaS).
self_serve_notes: Magento Open Source is free to self-host and generate integration tokens instantly; the commercial Adobe Commerce (cloud/SaaS) product requires a paid license and IMS-based OAuth2.

api_surface.notes: Extensive REST endpoints for products, orders, customers, inventory, admin tokens, and integrations; GraphQL also available for storefront use cases.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No dedicated Magento/Adobe Commerce toolkit found on Composio.

main_blocker: must self-host Magento Open Source or pay for Adobe Commerce license to get full SaaS auth

name: Squarespace
one_liner: Website builder with commerce features for stores, inventory, and orders.
self_serve_notes: Full Commerce APIs (Orders, Inventory, Transactions) require a paid Commerce/Core-tier-or-above plan; there is no free developer sandbox for commerce endpoints.

api_surface.notes: Covers Orders, Inventory, Products, Transactions, Profiles, Contacts, Websites, Webhooks — moderate breadth but limited compared to dedicated ecommerce platforms.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No Composio Squarespace toolkit page found.

main_blocker: requires a paid Commerce plan to unlock API access

name: Ecwid
one_liner: Ecommerce widget/storefront platform (by Lightspeed) embeddable on any site.
self_serve_notes: Any Ecwid account holder can create a custom app instantly at the Develop Apps page and receive Client ID/Secret plus non-expiring access tokens with no approval step.

api_surface.notes: REST API covers products, orders, customers, categories, discounts, and store profile; public vs. secret token scoping.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists 'Ecwid by Lightspeed' as an integration/tool.

name: Gumroad
one_liner: Simple platform for creators to sell digital products and memberships.
self_serve_notes: Anyone can create a free Gumroad account, register an application, and either generate a personal access token or run the OAuth2 flow instantly.

api_surface.notes: V2 REST API covers products, sales, subscribers, licenses, and user profile — a fairly small surface relative to full ecommerce platforms.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio has a dedicated Gumroad toolkit with 7 tools (OAuth2).

name: Amazon Selling Partner
one_liner: API for managing Amazon seller/vendor listings, orders, and fulfillment.
self_serve_notes: Requires registering as an SP-API developer (identity verification), an active Amazon seller/vendor account, and app approval that can take from minutes to weeks.

api_surface.notes: Covers orders, listings, inventory, fulfillment, reports, pricing, finances across many domain-specific APIs.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No Composio Amazon Selling Partner toolkit found.

main_blocker: requires seller/vendor account plus Amazon app approval (days to weeks)

name: fanbasis
one_liner: Payments/checkout infrastructure for creators to sell products and subscriptions.
self_serve_notes: API access is bundled into an existing FanBasis merchant dashboard rather than an independent developer signup; you must already be an onboarded FanBasis merchant, involving an approval/underwriting step typical of payments platforms.

api_surface.notes: Covers checkout sessions, customers, subscriptions, discount codes, webhooks, and refunds/disputes; SDKs for Node, Python, PHP, Ruby.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No Composio toolkit or official MCP server found for fanbasis; public API docs do exist at apidocs.fan and dev-docs.fanbasis.com.

main_blocker: requires becoming an approved FanBasis merchant before API keys are issued

name: DataForSEO
one_liner: SEO/SERP/keyword/backlink data platform with dozens of API endpoints across search engines.
self_serve_notes: Offers a free unlimited trial plus a sandbox/API playground before any paid commitment.

api_surface.notes: Dozens of endpoints across SERP, Keywords, Labs, OnPage, Backlinks, Domain Analytics, Merchant/App and Business Data APIs.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a DataForSEO toolkit with 312 tools; DataForSEO also promotes MCP integration.

name: SE Ranking
one_liner: SEO platform API for rank tracking, keyword research, backlinks, audits, and AI search visibility.
self_serve_notes: API is bundled into every subscription including the free trial account, though heavy usage needs a paid add-on.

api_surface.notes: 194 operations across 21 resources: keyword research, backlinks, domain analysis, audits, AI search, project management.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No Composio toolkit page found (404); SE Ranking advertises its own MCP integration for ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini.

main_blocker: No Composio toolkit yet; heavier use requires paid API add-on

name: Ahrefs
one_liner: SEO intelligence platform API for backlinks, keywords, rank tracking, and site audits.
self_serve_notes: Only limited free test queries are available without an Enterprise plan; full API access is gated to higher-tier/Enterprise subscriptions.

api_surface.notes: Covers Site Explorer, Rank Tracker, Site Audit, Keywords Explorer, and Brand Radar data.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists an Ahrefs MCP/toolkit with 40 tools, requiring the user's own Ahrefs API key.

name: MrScraper
one_liner: AI-assisted web scraping API with rotating/residential proxies and browser interaction.
self_serve_notes: Free trial tier with free trial tokens and no credit card required; tiered concurrency plans beyond that.

api_surface.notes: Scraper API plus SDKs for Python/Node/LangChain and an API playground; smaller product than platforms like Apify/Bright Data.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No Composio toolkit page found (404) and web search found no confirmation of a Composio or third-party MCP server.

main_blocker: No existing Composio/MCP toolkit; would need custom integration

name: Apify
one_liner: Cloud platform to run web scraping/automation "Actors" via a broad REST API.
self_serve_notes: Instant self-serve API token from the console; public Actors usable without auth, private resources need a token (free tier available).

api_surface.notes: Full OpenAPI spec covering Actors, tasks, builds, runs, datasets, key-value stores, request queues, webhooks, schedules, logs.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio hosts an Apify toolkit with 112 tools; Apify also ships its own official MCP server.

name: Firecrawl
one_liner: Web scraping/crawling API that turns sites into clean markdown/structured data for LLMs.
self_serve_notes: Free tier usable without an API key at lower rate limits; API key signup unlocks higher limits instantly.

api_surface.notes: Search, Scrape, Crawl, map, batch scrape, agent/browser actions, and structured extraction endpoints.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio toolkit with 29 tools; Firecrawl also ships an official MCP server for Claude/Cursor/Windsurf/VS Code.

name: Bright Data
one_liner: Web data platform providing proxies, SERP API, Web Unlocker, and scraping browser access.
self_serve_notes: Official MCP server offers a free plan (5,000 requests/month) with self-serve API tokens.

api_surface.notes: Covers Web Unlocker, SERP API, Scraping Browser, marketplace datasets, and Scrapers/Functions.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio toolkit with 10 tools; Bright Data ships its own official MCP server (@brightdata/mcp).

name: Sherlock
one_liner: Open-source CLI tool that hunts for usernames across 400+ social networks (OSINT).
self_serve_notes: Free open-source install via pip/pipx/Docker; no account or credentials of any kind.

api_surface.notes: Purely a local CLI (Python) with file-based outputs (CSV/XLSX/JSON/TXT); no hosted REST/GraphQL API exists.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No official hosted API to wrap; any agent integration would need to shell out to the CLI locally.

main_blocker: No hosted API; only usable by wrapping the local CLI binary as a tool

name: Waterfall.io
one_liner: B2B contact/company data API aggregating 30+ vendors for prospecting and enrichment.
self_serve_notes: Public API and docs exist but sign-up flow/pricing is not self-serve documented publicly; docs link returned 404 and no free-trial/instant-key process could be confirmed.

api_surface.notes: Endpoints for Prospector, Contact/Phone/Company Enrichment, and Email Verification, with async/webhook workflows.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No evidence found of a Composio toolkit or third-party MCP server for Waterfall.io.

main_blocker: No confirmed self-serve signup/instant API key; docs link broken and access process unclear

name: Clay
one_liner: Go-to-market data enrichment platform combining 100+ providers into spreadsheet-like tables.
auth_methods: Other: JWT (for HTTP API integrations within tables)
self_serve_notes: Free plan (1,200 credits/year) and a 14-day trial include HTTP API/webhook access; API key is obtained instantly from account settings.

api_surface.notes: Clay-native API key supports table lookups plus a Public API/CLI and inbound webhooks; broader capability is via its in-table HTTP API integration feature rather than a large standalone platform API.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No Composio toolkit found (404 and search turned up nothing); no confirmed official MCP server.

main_blocker: No existing Composio/MCP toolkit; native API surface is narrow (webhooks/table ops)

name: GitHub
one_liner: Git hosting platform for code, issues, pull requests, and CI/CD workflows.
self_serve_notes: Any developer can create a free personal access token (fine-grained or classic) or register an OAuth/GitHub App immediately, no approval needed.

api_surface.notes: Covers repos, issues, PRs, actions, packages, security alerts, orgs, and more across hundreds of endpoints plus a full GraphQL v4 API.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: CORRECTED after verification: Composio's GitHub toolkit page shows 846 tools/46 triggers (initial agent pass estimated ~893); GitHub also ships its own official remote/local MCP server (github/github-mcp-server).

name: Vercel
one_liner: Frontend cloud platform for deploying, hosting, and scaling web apps.
auth_methods: Other: OAuth2 (Sign in with Vercel / integrations)
self_serve_notes: Developers create personal Access Tokens directly from account settings on the free Hobby plan with no approval step.

api_surface.notes: REST API (api.vercel.com) covers deployments, projects, domains, env vars, teams, edge config, and firewall/WAF functionality.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio's Vercel toolkit reportedly exposes 170+ tools; no clearly documented first-party Vercel MCP server was confirmed in official docs.

name: Netlify
one_liner: Platform for building, deploying, and hosting static sites and serverless functions.
self_serve_notes: Personal access tokens are generated free and instantly from account settings on Netlify's free tier.

api_surface.notes: Covers sites, deploys, env vars, forms, DNS/SSL, webhooks, and serverless functions, documented via an OpenAPI spec.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio's Netlify integration page lists 0 tools/triggers despite auth methods being defined, effectively meaning no functioning toolkit exists yet; no official Netlify MCP server found.

main_blocker: No functioning Composio toolkit yet; would need to build directly against Netlify's REST API.

name: Cloudflare
one_liner: Global edge network for DNS, CDN, security (WAF), and serverless compute (Workers).
self_serve_notes: Developers self-serve API tokens (recommended) or legacy API keys directly from the dashboard on the free plan.

api_surface.notes: Over 2,500 endpoints spanning DNS, Workers, R2, KV, Zero Trust, WAF, load balancing, and more.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio offers Cloudflare toolkits; Cloudflare also ships official remote MCP servers covering Workers, R2, KV, D1, DNS, and the full API.

name: Supabase
one_liner: Open-source Firebase alternative: Postgres database, auth, storage, and edge functions.
self_serve_notes: Personal access tokens for the Management API are self-generated from the free-tier dashboard/account page.

api_surface.notes: Management API covers projects, API keys, auth providers, database webhooks, branching, and more; separate per-project REST/GraphQL data APIs also exist.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio's Supabase toolkit exposes 84+ tools; Supabase also maintains its own official MCP server.

name: Neo4j
one_liner: Native graph database platform for storing and querying connected data.
self_serve_notes: Neo4j Aura offers a free tier; Aura API credentials (OAuth client id/secret) can be created self-serve from the Aura console on any tier including free.

api_surface.notes: Aura API mainly manages instance lifecycle (create/pause/resume/delete databases); actual data access is via Cypher/Bolt protocol, not a broad REST surface.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a Neo4j toolkit (~210 actions); Neo4j also ships an official MCP server plus community neo4j-contrib/mcp-neo4j, supporting Aura, self-managed, and Docker deployments.

name: Snowflake
one_liner: Cloud data warehouse for SQL analytics, data sharing, and AI/Cortex workloads.
auth_methods: Other: key-pair (JWT) and workload identity federation
self_serve_notes: A no-credit-card 30-day trial with $400 credits lets a developer self-serve an account and generate OAuth/key-pair credentials, though production use requires converting to paid.

api_surface.notes: SQL API for executing statements plus separate REST APIs for account/object management, Cortex AI agents, and more.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio has snowflake toolkits (Basic Auth, 15 tools); Snowflake also runs an official managed MCP server plus open-source Snowflake-Labs/mcp.

name: MongoDB Atlas
one_liner: Fully-managed, multi-cloud MongoDB database-as-a-service platform.
auth_methods: Other: HTTP Digest auth (public/private API keys)
self_serve_notes: Atlas free tier (M0 clusters) lets developers self-generate Administration API keys from the dashboard with no sales contact.

api_surface.notes: Atlas Administration API covers projects, clusters, users, network access, backups, and more per its full OpenAPI spec.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No dedicated Composio 'MongoDB Atlas' toolkit was confirmed; MongoDB itself ships an official mongodb-js/mongodb-mcp-server supporting Atlas.

main_blocker: No confirmed dedicated Composio toolkit for Atlas admin API; would need to build directly against MongoDB's REST API or use its official MCP server instead.

name: Datadog
one_liner: Cloud observability platform unifying metrics, logs, traces, and security monitoring.
auth_methods: Other: Application Key (paired with API key)
self_serve_notes: A self-serve 14-day free trial lets a developer generate API/App keys immediately, but full ongoing use requires a paid plan afterward.

api_surface.notes: Covers dashboards, monitors, SLOs, hosts, downtimes, synthetics, logs, and more with 40+ tool categories in Composio's mapping.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio's Datadog toolkit has 40+ tools; Datadog also runs an official managed remote MCP server plus open-source datadog-labs/mcp-server.

main_blocker: Full platform access requires converting the 14-day trial to a paid plan.

name: Sentry
one_liner: Application error tracking and performance monitoring platform for developers.
self_serve_notes: Personal auth tokens are created free and instantly under User Settings on Sentry's free Developer tier.

api_surface.notes: 20+ endpoint categories covering issues, events, releases, dashboards, monitors/cron, teams, and SCIM provisioning.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio's Sentry toolkit has 200+ tools (OAuth-based); Sentry also runs its own official remote MCP server (mcp.sentry.dev) with OAuth, no manual API key setup needed.

name: Notion
one_liner: All-in-one workspace for notes, docs, wikis, and databases.
self_serve_notes: Developers create an internal integration token instantly for free at notion.so/my-integrations, or use OAuth2 for public integrations.

api_surface.notes: Broad REST API covering pages, databases, blocks, users, comments, and search.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a Notion toolkit with 45 tools/13 triggers; Notion also has a well-known official/community MCP server.

name: Airtable
one_liner: Cloud spreadsheet-database hybrid for building custom apps and workflows.
self_serve_notes: Personal access tokens can be generated instantly and free in account settings; legacy API keys were deprecated Feb 2024.

api_surface.notes: Full REST API for bases, tables, records, fields, comments, and attachments.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists an Airtable toolkit with 23 tools/6 triggers, supporting OAuth2 and API Key auth.

name: Linear
one_liner: Issue tracking and project management tool built for software teams.
self_serve_notes: Personal API keys are generated free and instantly in workspace settings; OAuth2 available for public apps.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a Linear toolkit (32 tools/3 triggers); Linear also has a known official MCP server.

name: Jira
one_liner: Issue and project tracking software for agile software development teams.
self_serve_notes: A free Jira Cloud site plus API token can be created instantly via id.atlassian.com without sales contact.

api_surface.notes: Extensive REST v3 API covering issues, projects, workflows, JQL search, and admin operations.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a Jira toolkit (94 tools/3 triggers); Atlassian's official remote MCP server went GA Feb 2026 covering Jira/Confluence/Bitbucket via OAuth2.1 or API tokens.

name: Asana
one_liner: Work management platform for tracking tasks, projects, and team goals.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists an Asana toolkit (153 tools/6 triggers); Asana's own developer docs describe an official MCP Server and compatible clients.

name: Monday.com
one_liner: Work operating system for building custom project and workflow apps.

api_surface.notes: GraphQL API covering boards, items, columns, groups, automations, and users.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a Monday.com toolkit (121 tools) with OAuth2; monday.com also promotes its own MCP/agent offering.

name: ClickUp

api_surface.notes: Broad REST API for tasks, lists, folders, spaces, docs, and time tracking.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a ClickUp toolkit (162 tools) with OAuth2/API Key; ClickUp also runs its own official MCP server (mcp.clickup.com) in public beta.

name: Coda

self_serve_notes: API is bearer-token authenticated and free to all users on free and paid workspaces; brand recently folded into 'Superhuman Docs' but API/docs still live.

api_surface.notes: RESTful API for docs, tables, rows, formulas, and automations, now documented at docs.superhuman.com.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a Coda toolkit (109 tools/4 triggers); Superhuman Docs' own site references a Superhuman Docs MCP server.

name: Smartsheet

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No Composio Smartsheet toolkit found (404 on toolkit page); no official MCP server confirmed.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a Harvest toolkit (57 tools) using OAuth2; no official Harvest MCP server found.

name: Stripe

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a Stripe toolkit (415 tools); Stripe also runs an official first-party MCP server at mcp.stripe.com.

name: Plaid

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No Plaid toolkit found in Composio catalog (404); Plaid itself runs an official Dashboard MCP server for account diagnostics/analytics, not general Plaid API access.

name: Binance

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No Binance toolkit found in Composio catalog (404, has CoinMarketCap instead); several community-built Binance MCP servers exist on GitHub.

name: Paygent Connect
one_liner: Payment gateway product name; no distinct public developer API/docs could be confirmed.
self_serve_notes: No public developer documentation site for a product named 'Paygent Connect' was found; NMI (its rumored underlying gateway) and the Japanese Paygent Co. gateway both require a merchant/partner relationship to get credentials.

api_surface.notes: Could not locate official public API reference docs specifically for 'Paygent Connect'; related NMI and Paygent Co. gateways gate access behind onboarding.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No Composio toolkit or MCP server found; flagged as a long-tail unknown with thin public docs, requiring human verification.

name: iPayX
one_liner: FX forensic audit tool scoring cross-border payment/FX fees and routing.
self_serve_notes: Free-tier API key (10 audits/day) is obtainable directly from the iPayX dashboard, no sales contact needed.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: iPayX ships its own official MCP server ('mcp-fx-audit'); no Composio toolkit found — flagged as a thin-docs long-tail app.

name: QuickBooks

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a QuickBooks toolkit with 105 tools using OAuth2.

name: Xero

api_surface.notes: Covers accounting, payroll, assets, projects, and reporting endpoints.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a Xero toolkit with 41 tools using OAuth2.

name: Brex

self_serve_notes: Requires being an existing Brex account admin or card admin who accepts the developer API agreement before generating a token; not open to arbitrary developers without a Brex account.

api_surface.notes: Covers cards, transactions, expenses, budgets, statements, and user/team management.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a Brex toolkit with 84 tools supporting OAuth2 and API Key.

main_blocker: requires an existing Brex company account/admin

name: Ramp
one_liner: Corporate card and spend management API for transactions, cards, and reimbursements.
self_serve_notes: Existing Ramp customers can create an OAuth app (client credentials grant) from Settings > Developer, but a separate sandbox environment requires contacting an account manager for approval.

api_surface.notes: Covers transactions, cards, users, departments, bills, reimbursements, accounting integrations, and webhooks.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a Ramp toolkit with 88 tools using OAuth2.

main_blocker: requires existing paying Ramp customer account plus AM approval for sandbox

name: PitchBook
one_liner: Private capital markets research platform providing deal, investor, and fund data.
self_serve_notes: PitchBook API/Direct Data is a separately contracted enterprise product; access requires being a PitchBook client and requesting API/sandbox keys through an account manager.

api_surface.notes: RESTful API covering deals, investors, funds, people, LPs, and service providers, gated behind a paid contract.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No dedicated PitchBook toolkit found on Composio's site or in catalog search.

main_blocker: enterprise contract/account-manager gated, no free public API tier

name: NotebookLM
one_liner: AI research assistant that summarizes and reasons over uploaded documents into notebooks/audio overviews.
self_serve_notes: Only the enterprise variant (NotebookLM Enterprise) exposes an API, and it requires a Google Cloud project, a Gemini Enterprise/Education Premium license, and admin console setup; the consumer product at notebooklm.google has no public API.

api_surface.notes: NotebookLM Enterprise API covers notebook CRUD, source management, and audio-overview generation, but is scoped to enterprise GCP tenants, not self-serve developers.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: NotebookLM appears only on Composio's tool-request board (open feature request, not a shipped toolkit); no evidence of a built Composio or third-party MCP server.

main_blocker: no self-serve API; enterprise/admin-gated Google Cloud product

name: Otter AI
one_liner: AI meeting transcription and notetaking tool with searchable meeting intelligence.
self_serve_notes: Otter's official Public API and MCP Server are both restricted to Enterprise workspaces; the API is not documented as available on free/lower tiers.

api_surface.notes: api.otter.ai/v1 exposes channels, conversations, transcripts, action items, insights and webhooks, but is gated to Enterprise; the MCP server exposes a similar narrow read/search surface, also OAuth-authenticated and enterprise-oriented.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Otter ships an official MCP Server; community/unofficial MCP wrappers also exist on GitHub; no Composio toolkit found.

main_blocker: official API/MCP gated behind Enterprise plan

name: Fathom
one_liner: AI meeting recorder that transcribes, summarizes, and syncs calls via API and webhooks.
self_serve_notes: Users generate an API key directly from User Settings > API Access with no application/approval step.

api_surface.notes: Covers meeting/webhook management (create/delete webhooks, receive meeting summary/transcript/action-item payloads) with HMAC signature verification; not a broad platform.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio hosts a Fathom MCP/direct-API toolkit with OAuth handling for Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, etc.

name: Consensus
one_liner: AI-powered search engine for scientific research papers with consensus/agreement scoring.
self_serve_notes: No account needed to try the MCP server; a free account can generate API credentials from the webapp's Integrations panel, though unlimited-rate enterprise access requires contacting Consensus directly.

api_surface.notes: API/MCP centers on paper search, summarization, and the 'Consensus Meter'; documented via an official MCP server and OpenAPI reference.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Consensus runs its own official MCP server; no confirmed Composio-built toolkit page (404).

name: Reducto
one_liner: Document parsing/extraction API that converts PDFs and complex documents into structured data.
self_serve_notes: Free account signup at studio.reducto.ai, then self-serve API key creation from the dashboard with no approval step.

api_surface.notes: REST API plus Python/Node/Go SDKs covering parsing, extraction, OCR, table/structure detection for agentic document workflows.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: CORRECTED after verification: docs.composio.dev/toolkits/reducto explicitly shows 'Composio Managed App Available? N/A' — no working Composio toolkit yet (initial agent pass had misread this as existing). An independent community mcp-server-reducto package exists.

name: Devin
one_liner: Autonomous AI software engineer that codes, reviews PRs, and manages agentic dev sessions.
self_serve_notes: Self-serve signup at app.devin.ai with a Free plan, but MCP/API integrations (Slack, Linear, MCP) are called out as Pro-plan-and-above features, so full programmatic access effectively needs a paid tier.

api_surface.notes: Devin MCP server and REST API cover session creation/control, playbooks, org knowledge base, scheduling, and repo/documentation querying.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Devin ships an official hosted MCP server; Composio also lists a 'Devin MCP' toolkit with OAuth/key management.

name: higgsfield
one_liner: AI image/video/3D-asset generation platform accessed via CLI, SDK, and hosted MCP.
self_serve_notes: Developers get API credentials via cloud.higgsfield.ai and the Python SDK/CLI; usage is credit-based (paid generation) though initial access/signup is self-serve.

api_surface.notes: Cloud API + Python SDK + official hosted MCP server expose 40+ image/video/audio/3D generation models; docs discourage calling the raw API directly and recommend the CLI/MCP wrapper.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Higgsfield is listed in Composio's toolkit catalog (~30 tools) and also runs its own official hosted MCP server independent of Composio.

name: Mermaid CLI
one_liner: Open-source command-line tool that renders Mermaid diagram text into SVG/PNG/PDF images.
auth_methods: Other: none (open-source local CLI; no accounts or keys)
self_serve_notes: Fully open-source npm package (@mermaid-js/mermaid-cli), installable and runnable locally with no signup; the related mermaid.ink hosted image API is also open/keyless.

api_surface.notes: No hosted, authenticated REST/OAuth service from the maintainers — it's a local CLI/Node.js API for rendering diagrams; mermaid.ink is a separate community-run keyless HTTP wrapper, not an official product API.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No Composio toolkit found; some community MCP servers exist for Mermaid diagram generation but not an official one.

main_blocker: CLI-only tool, no hosted OAuth/API service to integrate against

name: YouTube Transcript

self_serve_notes: Free API key obtained instantly at transcriptapi.com/signup with 100 free credits, then metered/paid usage beyond that.

api_surface.notes: Covers transcript retrieval, video search, channel browsing, and playlist extraction; small, single-purpose wrapper API (not an official YouTube product).

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: TranscriptAPI runs its own MCP server; Composio's 'YouTube' toolkit wraps the official YouTube Data API instead, not this transcript-specific service.

name: Grain
one_liner: AI meeting recording and note-taking tool with searchable conversation intelligence.
self_serve_notes: Personal/Workspace Access Tokens can be generated self-serve from account Settings > Integrations > API; OAuth2 app credentials setup is less clearly self-serve but the token-based path requires no sales contact.

api_surface.notes: Grain API v2 covers OAuth2, recordings (CRUD, transcripts, downloads, tagging, sharing), webhooks/hooks, users/teams, and meeting types, rate-limited to 300 req/min.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No confirmed Composio toolkit for Grain was found — search results repeatedly surfaced Composio's similarly-named 'Granola' toolkit instead, a distinct product; no dedicated Grain MCP server found.
